project: Stock Prediction Platform — FRED Timeseries Charts & Dashboard Macro View
description: Add realistic variance to synthetic FRED data, expose /market/macro/history API, build MacroChart timeseries component, and redesign Dashboard with Market/Macro toggle where Macro View shows latest-value cards + interactive FRED timeseries charts. Every story verified via Playwright MCP.

userStories.title: Create feast_yfinance_macro and populate VIX/SPY data
acceptanceCriteria: Already done

userStories.title: Create macro_fred_daily and populate FRED data
acceptanceCriteria: Already done
userStories.notes: macro_fred_daily has 90 flat synthetic rows (2026-01-05 to 2026-04-04).

userStories.title: Add realistic daily variance to macro_fred_daily synthetic timeseries
userStories.description: As a user, I need the 90-day FRED timeseries to have realistic day-to-day variation so charts show meaningful movement rather than flat lines.
acceptanceCriteria: Update all 90 rows in macro_fred_daily with realistic variation using a Python script via kubectl exec or direct psql UPDATE with generate_series math
dgs10: trend from ~4.55 in Jan to ~4.35 in Apr with ±0.05 daily noise
dgs2: trend from ~5.00 to ~4.82 with ±0.04 daily noise
t10y2y: range -0.20 to -0.65 with ±0.03 daily noise
bamlh0a0hym2 (HY spread): spike to ~345 in Feb, recover to ~300, ±12 noise
dcoilwtico (WTI): range 65-78 with ±2 daily noise
icsa (jobless claims): range 205000-230000 with weekly pattern
All other series (t10y3m, dbaa, t10yie, dtwexbgs, dexjpus, nfci, cpiaucsl, pcepilfe): add ±2% noise around base value
Verify: SELECT as_of_date, dgs10, dgs2, dcoilwtico FROM macro_fred_daily ORDER BY as_of_date LIMIT 5 — values must differ row by row
curl http://localhost:8010/market/macro/latest still returns non-null values
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Updated all 90 rows via psql UPDATE with CTE+trig noise. dgs10 4.566→4.393, t10y2y -0.19→-0.668, HY spread spiked to 352.5 in Feb. /market/macro/latest returns updated values (dgs10=4.393, baml_hy_oas=292.7).

userStories.title: Add GET /market/macro/history endpoint and fix /market/macro/latest column mapping
userStories.description: As a frontend developer, I need a /market/macro/history?days=90 endpoint returning the FRED timeseries as a JSON array, and the /market/macro/latest endpoint must correctly map all column names.
acceptanceCriteria: Add MacroHistoryPoint Pydantic schema to schemas.py: as_of_date (date), plus all 14 FRED fields as Optional[float]
Add get_macro_history(days: int = 90) to market_service.py: SELECT * FROM macro_fred_daily ORDER BY as_of_date ASC LIMIT {days}
Add GET /market/macro/history route to market.py: query param days=90, returns List[MacroHistoryPoint], 5-min Redis cache
Fix get_macro_latest() column mapping: bamlh0a0hym2->baml_hy_oas, dcoilwtico->wti_crude, dtwexbgs->usd_broad — verify MacroLatestResponse field names match
Rebuild stock-api: eval $(minikube docker-env) && docker build -t stock-api:latest stock-prediction-platform/services/api/ && kubectl rollout restart deployment/stock-api -n ingestion
curl 'http://localhost:8010/market/macro/history?days=10' returns 10-item JSON array with varying values
curl 'http://localhost:8010/market/macro/latest' returns as_of_date non-null + at least 8 non-null numeric fields
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Added MacroHistoryPoint schema (15 FRED fields). Added get_macro_history() service. Added GET /market/macro/history?days=90 route with 5-min Redis cache. Column mapping in get_macro_latest() was already correct. Rebuilt stock-api image and rolled out. Verified: /market/macro/history?days=10 returns 10 items with row-by-row varying values; /market/macro/latest returns as_of_date=2026-04-04 with 11 non-null fields.

userStories.title: Add fetchMacroHistory API client and useMacroHistory hook to frontend
userStories.description: As a frontend developer, I need typed API client and React hook for /market/macro/history so the chart can consume timeseries data.
acceptanceCriteria: Add MacroHistoryPoint TypeScript interface to frontend/src/api/types.ts: as_of_date: string, plus all 14 FRED fields as number | null
Add fetchMacroHistory(days?: number) to frontend/src/api/client.ts: fetches /api/macro/history?days={days||90}
Add useMacroHistory(days?: number) hook returning { data: MacroHistoryPoint[], loading, error }
Export both from frontend/src/api/index.ts
TypeScript 0 errors, npm run build succeeds
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Added MacroHistoryPoint (15 fields) to types.ts, fetchMacroHistory() to client.ts, useMacroHistory hook to src/hooks/useMacroHistory.ts. Exported fetchMacroHistory and useMacroHistory from api/index.ts. TypeScript 0 errors, npm run build succeeded.

userStories.title: Build MacroChart timeseries component with series selector
userStories.description: As a user, I want an interactive Recharts line chart showing selected FRED indicators over 90 days with a series picker.
acceptanceCriteria: Create frontend/src/components/dashboard/MacroChart.tsx
Uses Recharts ResponsiveContainer + LineChart

SERIES_CONFIG array: dgs10 ('10Y Yield','#4fc3f7','%'), t10y2y ('2-10 Spread','#f06292','%'), dgs2 ('2Y Yield','#81c784','%'), bamlh0a0hym2 ('HY Spread','#ffb74d','bps'), dcoilwtico ('WTI Crude','#ce93d8','$'), icsa ('Jobless Claims','#80cbc4','K')
Series picker: MUI Chip row, default selected: dgs10, t10y2y, dcoilwtico
Each selected series = one Line with its color, dot=false, strokeWidth=2
X-axis: as_of_date formatted MMM DD, every 2 weeks
Tooltip: shows date + values for all selected series with units
Loading: Skeleton height=300px. Error: muted text.
TypeScript 0 errors, npm run build succeeds
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Dashboard Market/Macro toggle: Market View = stocks, Macro View = cards + timeseries chart
userStories.description: As a user, I want a clean toggle on the Dashboard separating stock data from macro data so it is not confusing.
acceptanceCriteria: Add MUI ToggleButtonGroup ('Market' | 'Macro') to Dashboard.tsx below the PageHeader, above all content
Market View (default): existing sections only — ticker strip, treemap, top gainers/losers, sentiment panel. No MacroPanel, no MacroChart.
Macro View: heading 'Macro Environment', MacroPanel (compact 3-col grid of 9 cards), then heading 'Historical Trends — 90 Days', then MacroChart
Remove MacroPanel from bottom of Market View entirely
useState<'market' | 'macro'>('market') in Dashboard.tsx
TypeScript 0 errors, npm run build succeeds
Navigate to http://localhost:5173/ via Playwright MCP
Take screenshot dashboard-market-tab.png — treemap visible, no macro cards
Click Macro button via mcp__playwright__browser_click
Take screenshot dashboard-macro-tab.png — 9 cards + timeseries chart visible
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
Typecheck passes
